_**User (2026-04-28 10:19:10Z)**_

Why is  .claude/settings.local.json still showing if I merged to have as .gitignore?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 10:19:13Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 10:19:16Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Check if file is tracked and gitignore status

`git ls-files --error-unmatch .claude/settings.local.json 2>&1; echo "---"; grep -n "settings.local" .gitignore 2>&1; echo "---"; git log --oneline -5 -- .gitignore .claude/settings.local.json`

```text
.claude/settings.local.json
---
25:.claude/settings.local.json
---
a4c66b86 chore(gitignore): also ignore *.code-workspace
880298f7 chore(gitignore): ignore .claude/settings.local.json
db8fe95b chore(mcp): drop storybook MCP from committed config
81db0d46 fix(db): recover from broken DM4 migrations on boot
e543a89d chore(e2e/VR): update VR tests changes with docker fixes
```

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-04-28 10:19:21Z)**_

Because `.gitignore` only ignores **untracked** files. `.claude/settings.local.json` was already committed to the repo before commit `880298f7` added it to `.gitignore`, so git keeps tracking it.

To stop tracking it (without deleting the file locally):

```bash
git rm --cached .claude/settings.local.json
git commit -m "chore: untrack .claude/settings.local.json"
```

After that, the gitignore entry will take effect and your local edits won't show up in `git status` anymore.

Want me to do this on a worktree branch and open a PR?

---

_**User (2026-04-28 10:19:30Z)**_

Y

Commit the untrack change

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Open PR against master

```bash
gh pr create --base master --title "chore: untrack .claude/settings.local.json" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- `.claude/settings.local.json` was added to `.gitignore` in 880298f7, but the file was already tracked, so git kept reporting local edits in `git status`.
- Remove it from the index with `git rm --cached` so the existing ignore rule actually takes effect. The file stays on disk locally.

## Test plan

- [ ] After merge, pull master and verify `git status` no longer shows `.claude/settings.local.json` as modified
- [ ] Verify `git check-ignore -v .claude/settings.local.json` reports the `.gitignore:25` rule

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

https://github.com/leocaseiro/base-skill/pull/211

PR opened: <https://github.com/leocaseiro/base-skill/pull/211>

Once it merges, pull master locally and `.claude/settings.local.json` will stop showing in `git status` — the file stays on disk, just untracked.
